prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `7e576d6e`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `7e576d6e` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7e576d6e/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7e576d6e/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7e576d6e/generator.py), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7e576d6e/notes.md). The rule is: the least-common color is the wire, the second-least-common color marks 3-cell bars embedded in full parallel lines, and the output draws a one-cell orthogonal wire between the two markers through each bar center, ordered left-to-right for vertical-bar tasks and top-to-bottom for horizontal-bar tasks. No new primitive was needed.

I discarded the sonnet summary because it describes rectangles, which is incompatible with the official outputs. The opus summary was usable after correcting the ordering detail. The generator matches the family by explicitly sampling 3-4 full parallel lines, replacing one 3-cell segment per line with the bar color, placing the two wire markers on opposite sides, and constructing the routed wire directly rather than deriving it from the verifier.

Verification passed end-to-end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the previews. Artifacts are [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7e576d6e/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7e576d6e/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7e576d6e/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7e576d6e/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7e576d6e/previews/preview_03.png).
